categoryDefinitions.title: Category Definitions
categoryDefinitions.description: Start managing the category tree and category-linked stocks for B2B and B2C catalogs from here.
categoryDefinitions.guidedTitle: Manage the category structure in 3 clear steps
categoryDefinitions.hierarchyBlueprintDescription: If you keep the category tree in a fixed structure, the experience becomes clearer for users and product matching stays more reliable.
categoryDefinitions.hierarchyExampleValue: Phones > Feature Phones > iPhone > 17 Series > Product List
categoryDefinitions.easyModeTitle: You do not need to guess what to do here
categoryDefinitions.summaryGuideTitle: I will show you the next correct step
categoryDefinitions.createCatalogDescription: Add a new catalog definition for B2B, B2C, or custom flows.
categoryDefinitions.editCatalogDescription: Update the selected catalog's name, type, and ordering.
categoryDefinitions.deleteCatalogConfirm: Are you sure you want to delete the {{name}} catalog?
categoryDefinitions.deleteCategoryConfirm: Are you sure you want to delete the {{name}} category?
categoryDefinitions.deleteStockConfirm: Are you sure you want to remove this stock assignment?
categoryDefinitions.createRuleDescription: Create an automatic matching rule for the {{category}} category.
categoryDefinitions.editRuleDescription: Update the rule details for the {{category}} category.
categoryDefinitions.deleteRuleConfirm: Are you sure you want to delete the {{name}} rule?
categoryDefinitions.noRules: No rules have been defined for this category yet.
categoryDefinitions.catalogsDescription: Select the catalog you want to work with first.
categoryDefinitions.catalogDescriptionFallback: No description has been entered for this catalog yet.
categoryDefinitions.catalogHelperText: In short: choose a catalog on the left, move down the tree in the middle, manage stocks and rules on the right.
categoryDefinitions.treeDescription: Navigate from category to subcategory in a Trendyol or Hepsiburada style flow.
categoryDefinitions.treeReorderHint: You can reorder categories on the same level by dragging and dropping them.
categoryDefinitions.chooseCatalogFirst: First choose a catalog on the left. Category management does not start before that.
categoryDefinitions.stocksDescription: Stocks linked to the selected leaf category will be listed here.
categoryDefinitions.stocksGuideDescription: Here you decide which products belong to the selected category. You can assign stocks one by one if needed.
categoryDefinitions.stocksHelperText: Simple flow: first pick a category, then click 'Add Stock', then choose the product from the list.
categoryDefinitions.favoritesGuideDescription: Shows the stocks you like inside the selected category in a user-specific quick access list. Stock favorites and category favorites are kept separate.
categoryDefinitions.favoritesHelperText: This category has {{count}} favorite stocks. Click the heart again to remove a stock from favorites.
categoryDefinitions.rulesGuideTitle: What does the rule screen do?
categoryDefinitions.rulesGuideDescription: If you do not want to assign similar products one by one, define a rule here. For example, if Code1 = GLASS, put it into this category.
categoryDefinitions.rulesHelperText: First create the rule. Then use 'Preview Rules' to see what will happen. If it looks correct, use 'Apply Rules' to assign automatically.
categoryDefinitions.selectionDescription: Use the right panel to see the selected catalog, category, and linked stocks at a glance.
categoryDefinitions.nextStepChooseCatalog: First, choose a catalog from the left panel.
categoryDefinitions.nextStepBrowseTree: Use the center panel to move from main categories down to deeper levels.
categoryDefinitions.nextStepSelectLeaf: Select a leaf category to view the stock list.
categoryDefinitions.nextStepReviewStocks: Review the stocks linked to the selected category and confirm the match.
categoryDefinitions.stepCatalogDescription: Decide whether you are working in a B2B, B2C, or custom catalog.
categoryDefinitions.stepTreeDescription: Progress by clicking through main categories, subcategories, and lower levels.
categoryDefinitions.stepStocksDescription: Once a leaf category is selected, see linked stocks and ERP code breakdowns in the right panel.
categoryDefinitions.noCatalogs: No catalogs have been defined yet.
categoryDefinitions.noCategories: No categories were found at this level.
categoryDefinitions.noStocks: No stocks are linked to this category.
categoryDefinitions.noFavorites: No favorite stocks have been added to this category yet.
categoryDefinitions.selectLeafHint: Select a leaf category to view linked stocks.
categoryDefinitions.stockSearchPlaceholder: Search by stock code or stock name...
categoryDefinitions.favoriteSearchPlaceholder: Search favorites by stock code or name...

tips.catalogDescription: Even if category names look similar, B2B and B2C can require different catalog structures.
tips.branchDescription: Branch categories are not the final selection point. Use them to move users toward the correct lower level.
tips.leafTitle: 3. Validate stocks on the leaf category
tips.leafDescription: The linked stock list becomes meaningful at the leaf level, so the final review should happen there.
tips.exampleDescription: Recommended structure: Main Category > Subcategory > Brand > Series > Products. This helps the team use the same naming logic and makes search results more predictable.

root.title: Main Category
root.description: Represents the top business area. Example: Phones

subcategory.title: Subcategory
subcategory.description: Splits the product family into a clearer group. Example: Feature Phones

brand.title: Brand
brand.description: Use this level for brand or manufacturer breakdowns. Example: iPhone

series.title: Series
series.description: Keep model family or series-based distinctions here. Example: 17 Series

products.title: Products
products.description: This should be the final leaf level; stock assignments should be done here.

messages.categoryReorderError: Category order could not be updated.
messages.stockAssignError: Stock could not be assigned to category.
messages.stockUnassignError: Stock assignment could not be removed.
messages.favoriteToggleError: Favorite status could not be updated.
messages.rulePreviewError: Rule preview could not be loaded.
messages.stockHierarchyPreviewError: Stock hierarchy preview could not be loaded.
messages.stockHierarchyApplySuccess: Catalog tree generated from RII_STOK hierarchy.
messages.stockHierarchyApplyError: Catalog tree could not be generated from RII_STOK hierarchy.

stockHierarchyImport.title: Build catalog from RII_STOK hierarchy
stockHierarchyImport.description: Converts Group Code > Code1 > Code2 > Code3 into a catalog tree. Like marketplace category flows, users drill down step by step and see stocks at the final level.

includeCode1.title: Include Code1 level
includeCode1.description: Creates the first child split under Group Code.

includeCode2.title: Include Code2 level
includeCode2.description: Creates the second child split under Code1.

includeCode3.title: Include Code3 level
includeCode3.description: Creates the third child split under Code2. If empty, the deepest available level becomes the leaf.

assignStocks.title: Assign stocks to leaf level
assignStocks.description: Automatically links each stock to its deepest generated category.

categoryDefinitions.rulePreviewListEmpty: No sample matches were found for preview.

form.descriptionPlaceholder: Explain this definition briefly so other managers can understand it.
form.nodeTypeBranchHelp: A branch category is for navigation and deeper breakdowns. Do not attach products directly at this level.
form.nodeTypeLeafHelp: A leaf category is the final selection point. Stock matching and product listing should live at this level.
form.visualPresetHelp: You do not upload an external image here. The system creates the category look based on this choice.
form.categoryImageHelp: You can optionally upload a system-owned image. If you do, the category card uses this image instead of the preset icon.
form.ruleValueEmpty: No matching values were found for this field.
form.ruleValueDropdownHelp: The system lists real ERP values from the selected stock field. Choosing from here is safer than typing manually.
form.ruleValueInListHelp: With the In list operator, you can select multiple values. Pick from above, click a badge to remove it.
form.ruleValueManualHelp: This operator works with free text. Type the text to match for contains, starts with, or ends with.
form.ruleValueTopMatchesTitle: Most common values in the system
form.ruleValueTopMatchesEmpty: No highlighted values were found for this field.
